# signoz_route_policy (Resource)

Creates and manages route policies in SigNoz. A route policy decides which notification channels an alert should fan out to, based on a SigNoz expression evaluated against alert labels.

## Example Usage

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    signoz = {
      source = "registry.terraform.io/signoz/signoz"
    }
  }
}

provider "signoz" {
  endpoint     = "http://localhost:8080"
  access_token = "<SIGNOZ-API-KEY>"
}

resource "signoz_route_policy" "warnings" {
  name        = "warnings-to-test-channel"
  description = "Route warning-severity alerts to the test channel."
  expression  = "severity == \"warning\""
  channels    = ["test-channel", "second-channel"]
  kind        = "policy"
  tags        = ["acc-test", "v2"]
}

output "route_policy_id" {
  value = signoz_route_policy.warnings.id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->

## Schema

### Required

- `channels` (List of String) Notification channel names this policy routes matching alerts to.
- `expression` (String) SigNoz expression evaluated against alert labels. When the expression is true the alert is routed to `channels`.
- `name` (String) Name of the route policy. Used as the human-readable identifier in the UI.

### Optional

- `description` (String) Free-form description of the policy.
- `kind` (String) Policy kind. One of: `rule`, `policy`. Defaults to a server-chosen value.
- `tags` (List of String) Free-form tags applied to the policy.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Autogenerated unique ID for the route policy.
